ety: for A. حرفة ḥirfat, fem.; v.n. fr. حرف 'to turn to'
def: adj. Crafty, artful, cunning, intriguing
more: n. f. 1. Trade; profession; craft. 2. Skill. 3. Art; artifice ( chālākī , 2, 3, 4.). 4. Wickedness; villany. hirfat-bāz , adj. Cratty; artful; intriguing. Tū dadā, ek hai, Allāh rī! O hirfat-bāz! Qādrī māṅgī thī, tū dauṛ-ke lāī pishvāz! Wom. Rangīn. You have n't your fellow, nurse, O crafty artful one! I for my jacket called, you run and fetch my gown!
s. f. 1. Trade, art, profession, craft. 2. Skill, ingenuity, cleverness, cunning. 3. (dakh.) See مکر makr.
subs: s.m. Workmen, operatives, artizans; men of skill, artists

ety: inf. n. of حرص 'to desire eagerly'

To be avaricious; to covet. hirsā hirsī, hirsā hirs ; H. dekhā dekhī , adv. Covetously; greedily; avariciously. hirs dilānā, hirs denā , v. a. To entice; tempt; allure. hirs kā kuttā , n. m. 1. A greedy dog. 2. A slavish imitator.

ety: inf. n. of حسب, and also of iii of حسب 'to number,' &c.
def: s.m. A numbering, counting, reckoning, calculation, computation; arithmetic; account, accounts; bill (of charges); rate, price, charge; — measure, measurement; proportion; rule, standard; — estimation, judgment, opinion; — condition, category
more: n. m. حسب computing. 1. Accounts (اُچاپت 1). 2. Computation; calculation; cyphering; arithmetic. Yā le-ke ek qalamdāṅ, aur rakh qalam ko sir par, Joṛe hisāb lākhoṅ, chehre likhe barābar, Jab umr kī kachahrī jhāṅkī qazā ne ā-kar, Phir āp, na qalamdān, kāgaz rahā, na daftar, Munshī, vakīl, dīvāṅ mar mar huā to phir kyā? On death. Nazīr. Or pen and inkstand held in hand, a pen stuck in his ear, Computing lakhs , or entering names upon his register, When death looked in at life's commercial toil and busy stir, Nor self, nor pen and ink, nor paper, office there, Clerk, lawyer, and financier, hard-earned acquisitions where? 3. Rate; price; cost; charges; bill of charges. 4. Measure; measurement; rule; standard; proportion. 5. Estimation; opinion; judgment; view. 6. Condition ( hāl , 1.). 7. Method; way; manner. 8. Economy ( chalan , 5). 9. Carnal knowledge; illicit love ( āshnāī ). hisāb barāb…

def: s.m. Part, portion, lot, share, division; dividend; — class; compartment
more: n. m. Pl. hisas حص to divide. 1. A share; share in a partnership; a share of revenue or rent; dividend ( bāṅṭ , 1, 2.). 2. Class; compartment; department. 3. A part or division of a book. hissa-i-ausat . An average share. hissa-i-khurd . The lesser part. hisse-dār , One of a community of sharers; a copartner; coparcener; a partner in a business or firm; a sharer; shareholder; one who pays his share of the revenue either to a zamīndār or to the State. hisse-dārāṅ , Sharers; shareholders. hisse-dāroṅ kī jamāat . A joint-stock company. hisse-dāroṅ kī sanad , Bank scrip. hissā-dārī , n. Coparcenership, applied especially to a village in which a number of sharers have a joint proprietary right in the land. hissah-rasadī , A proportional share, part, or contribution; a fractional share of land; a dividend on a share; a rate; cess. hissah k. , v. a. To divide ( bhāg k .). hissa-kashī , n. f. Di…

ety: ho = Prk. होइ = S. भवति 'he (or it) is'+gā = gayā = Ap. Prk. गया = Prk. गमिअओ = S. गम+इत+क (गमितकः, for गतकः 'gone')
def: 3rd pers. sing. of the fut. indic. of honā (f. hogī), (He or it) will be; — it must be; may be, perhaps (cf. shāyad).
intj. See s.v. هو ho. part. 'Ho! hallo! I say!' (most com. placed after the name of the person addressed; — cf. hot)
n. f. A festival held eight days before Divālī.
s.f. Name of a festival held eight days before the Diwālī.

def: part. & adv. = ho-ke, q.v.
subs: To remain permanently or as a fixture; — to be inevitable.

def: adv. Through, by.
more: 1. past conjunctive part. (of हौनौं in Braj ) Having been. 2. adv. Through, by.

r: ho-ke, hū-kai, hwai-kai, honā, karnā, ho, ke
ety: conj. parts. of honā and karnā; see ho and ke
def: part. & adv. Having been; being; having become; having taken place; coming, going, passing; proceeding (from); — by way of, viâ; through; by means of, by, under the care of; — as.

n. m. S. होलक 1. (Bhoj. horahā ) The chick pea parched in the pod. 2. E. Green gram. 1. Rāhe ailī, rāhe gailī, khet baiṭh-ke horaho na khailī . Bhoj. Prov. I came this way, I went this way; no peas I plucked, in fields I made no stay. 2. Maiṅ lāūṅ sūṅ būṅṭ gādre, tauṅh jhaṛ ḍhīkar lā, sāthan, Hole kar-kar maiṅ bhī khāūn, tauṅh bhī mo saṅg khā, sāthan! Rus. I'll fetch green gram, do you some brambles bring, we 'll have a treat, We 'll parch the gram and I will eat, and you, my dear, shall eat. 3. A large flat-bottomed boat. (?) 4. The Sikh Holi , held a day after the orthodox festival.
s.m. (dialec.) corr. of olā, 'hail,' q.v. s.m. Hailstone, hail; refined sugar made up into balls; — adj. Cold (as hail)
more: s. m. The chickpea having been parched in the pod.
s. m. (dakh.) Hail: see اولا olā.
s. m. Name of a kind of boat (large and flat-bottomed).
subs: v.n. To be or become cold
v.n. To hail; to be overtaken by misfortune (joṅ sir munḍāyā toṅ ole paṛe, 'I had no sooner shaved my head than hail fell,' — i.e. Misfortune overtook my first venture).

ety: Prk. होडओ or होलओ (?); S. होड+कः, rt. होड्
def: s.m. (dialec.) A boat made out of a hollowed tree, a canoe; — a large flat-bottomed boat; a raft.

ety: S. होलकः
def: s.m. Chick-pea or 'gram' half-parched in the pod; (dialec.) chick-pea in the pod; green gram (cf. ćholā).

ety: see holī
def: s.m. (dialec.) The Sikh holī (held a day after the orthodox festival).

s.f. = هولي holī, q.v. s.f. The great spring festival of the Hindūs, held at the approach of the vernal equinox; — the pile of fuel prepared for burning the holī; — a class of songs sung during the festival
subs: To observe the holī festival; to sprinkle one another with red or yellow powder (abīr, or gulāl).

def: n. m. S. उल्वल 1. A child in the womb. Ā, jachchā-rānī, leṭ, malūṅ terā peṭ, holaṛ kahāṅ base! Song. Jachchāgīrī . Come, queen enceinte . Your stomach let me rub and feel where dwells the child! 2. (Sant. bāle gidra ) A new-born babe. 3. A class of songs sung at a birth.

ety: prob. Prk. उल्लडं; S. उल्व+रं
def: s.m. A fœtus; a child in the womb; — (dialec.) a new-born babe; — a class of songs sung on the occasion of the birth of a child.
v. n. To be completed.

ety: S. होलिका
def: s.f. The great spring festival of the Hindūs, held at the approach of the vernal equinox; — the pile of fuel prepared for burning the holī; — a class of songs sung during the festival
more: s. f. 1. The great festival held at the approach of the vernal equinox. 2. The song which is sung during the festival.
subs: To observe the holī festival; to sprinkle one another with red or yellow powder (abīr, or gulāl).

def: n. f. S. होलाका. 1. The great festival held at the approach of the vernal equinox in honor of the goddess Horkā . 1. Holī hai, kis bhaṛve kī chorī hai? It's Holi, we will merry be! Dare rascal cry it's robbery! (Let him cry out who's hurt!) 2. Hamre Kadar saṅg kheleṅ holī, joban madh mātiyāṅ, Raṅg kī pichkāriyāṅ gulāl bhare kumkume, Pheṅk pheṅk bhāg jātiyāṅ . Ṭhumrī. Kadar. I'll with my Qadar Holi play, my full breasts bursting are, Pink water squirting here, red powder balls in showers there, Discharge and pelt and run away the bouncing frisking fair. 2. The pile of fuel prepared for burning the Holi. 3. A class of songs sung during this festival. holī kā bhaṛvā , n. m. A guy set up in the Holī. holī khelnā , v. a. To throw abīr and gulāl on one another in the Holī festival. 1. Holī khelūṅ khasam ke hāṛ? ghar meṅ nāj nahīṅ! How Holī play! my husband's bones for a powder bruise? Not a grain in the house! 2. Holī khelan ko tū jāëgī, sakhī rī, kyā kyā karegī arī din ke din? Wom. Song Holī. My dear, when you go hence to Holī play, what will you be up to from day to day?
s.f. = holī, q.v.; — H. (also) होलिका holkā, s.f. The goddess who is supposed to preside over the Holī festival
n. f. The goddess Holī. Bolo re logo jaë horkā maiyā kī! Shout ye people victory to mother Holī!
n. f. Fried spiced gram. Karāre holū ke! Delhi street cry.

n. m. S. होम from हु burn. A kind of burnt-offering; the Hindu sacrificial offerings inculcated in the Veds. Clarified butter, etc. are thrown into the sacred fire as an offering to the gods, accompanied by prayers or invocations.

s.m. An oblation with clarified butter, a burnt-offering, a sacrifice; the casting clarified butter, &c. into fire as an offering to the gods, accompanied with invocations and prayers according to the objects of sacrifice
more: s. m. A kind of burnt-offering, the casting of clarified butter, &c. into the sacred fire as an offering to the gods, accompanied with prayers or invocations, according to the object of the sacrifice.
subs: s.m. One who performs the hom; — a fire-worshipper.

def: s.m. The priest who makes the oblation of clarified butter, &c.; — one who offers an oblation.

ety: fr. hom
def: v.t. To make the oblation (of clarified butter, &c.) which characterizes the hom.
more: v. a. To perform the sacrifice of the هوم hom.

ety: Ap. Prk. हउं, हमु; Prk. अहं, हअं; S. अहम्
def: pron. I
subs: or hauṅ-hūṅ, I too, I also (i.q. maiṅ-bhī).

def: v. n. S. भू P. būdan to be. 1. To exist; be. 1. Aisan suhāg morā nit uṭhi hoë! E. Prov. May coverture like this endure for aye! 2. Abdhū! nā kuchh gayā na āyā, Ādi hu hato, soī ab hūṅ hai, sarab sarūp samāyā . Kabīr. You fool! no thing whate'er hath come or gone, What was is now, all things His presence own. 2. To become; come to pass. 1. Na hone se honā behtar hai . Prov. Something is better than nothing. 3. To be born; come into existence. Aë huā beṭā? Eunuchs' cry. Ho there! is a son born anywhere? 4. To belong to; to have. Apnā haië nā, dusrā ke dānī! E. Prov. In want myself and give another! 5. To accrue; result. 6. To succeed. 7. To serve; answer. 8. To be over; come to an end. 9. To be with menses. 10. To discharge semen. Nauj karvāūṅ maiṅ aise se kabhī! Ṭāṅg lete hī nigoṛā ho gayä! Wom. Jān Sāhab. honā na-honā , n. m. Existence and non-existence; to be or not to be. Honā na honā Khudā ke hāth hai, pairavī karnā kām hamārā hai . To be or not to be in God's hands are, my part it is my work to do. hone-vālā, hon-hār . 1. Approaching; impending; imminent. 2. ( hone-jog ) Promising. Honhār ho-kar ṭale . Prov. What is to be will be.

def: adj. Existent, being.

ety: ho˚+anihār = Prk. अणिअअडं (with euphon. h inserted) = S. अनीय+क+रं
def: adj. What is to be or to happen; yet to be, yet to take place; of or belonging to futurity, future; — to be expected to result; eventual; likely; possible; — approaching, impending, imminent; — feasible; — promising, hopeful; — s.f. Appointed lot, destiny, fate.
more: adj. 1. What is to happen. 2. Possible, feasible, hopeful. هونہاربرواکي چکني چکني پات honhār birwā-ke chikne chikne pāt, A proverb applied to a promising child.

ety: honhār+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Possibility, likelihood; feasibility; hopefulness, promise.

n. f. What is to be; destiny. An-honī ke hon ko tākat haiṅ sab koë, An-honī honī nahīṅ, honī hoë so hoë . To come to pass what cannot be all wistful you may see, What's not to be willnever be, what is to be will be. ho-baiṭhnā , v. n. 1. To declare or profess oneself. Chār kitābeṅ paṛh-kar Mullā ho baiṭhe! He has read four books and he is a Doctor forsooth! 2. To have the menses. ho jānā , v. n. 1. ( ho chuknā ) To become; befall. 3. To be possessed by an evil spirit. Ise to kuchh ho gayā hai . 3. ( ho chuknā ) To come to an end; to be finished, done up. Qurāṅ uṭhāte haiṅ tama-i-zar ke vāste, Dīṅdār gar yehī haiṅ to Islām ho chukā . They take up their Kurān for pence on self to spend Rind. When such the pious are of Islam there's an end. ho chuknā , v. n. 1. To be all over with one; to die. Bachchā ho chukā . 2. To be one's slave; to stick to one. Tum hamāre ho na ho, ham tumhāre ho chuke . Prov. Be you my own or not, I am already yours. ho rahnā , v. n. To abide; stay; remain. Wahīṅ kā ho rahā. ho saknā , v. n. To be possible. ho-kar rahnā , v. n. To be inevitable. ho-ke , adv. Through; via ; under the care of. ho guzarnā , v. n. 1. To come to pass. 2. To be done; to be over. ho lenā , v. n. 1. To be completed. 2. To stick to a place. ho nikalnā , v. n. To pass; pass by. ho na ho . It must be so; necessarily; right or wrong. Ho na ho vohī ho. huā so huā . Let bygones be bygones. hotā jātā hai . Is becoming; in course of preparation. hotā rahegā, hotā sotā rahegā . It will fall or recoil on you. Tū yūṅ gāliyāṅ gair ko shauq se de! Hameṅ kuchh kahegā to hotā rahegā . Mīr. hote , adv. During; while. hotī āī hai . It has always been so from the beginning. Hotī āī hai ki achchhoṅ ko burā kahte haiṅ . Prov. It hath been so from the beginning that the good have e'er been evil called. hote par . During possession; while. hote sote , Wom. n. m. Relations; kinsfolk ( parvār ). 1. Apne hote sotoṅ ko koso! 2. Kahā 'hote sotoṅ se apne kaho, Faqīroṅ ko chheṛo na, baiṭhe raho!' Mir Hasan. hote sote, hote sāṅte, hote huāte, hote savāte , adv. 1. In the state of being, having, or possessing. 2. Possessing a competency. Kyoṅ hote sote lāteṅ chalāte ho? hai'gā, hoëgā , Brij. It must, shall, or will be.

def: n. m. S. ओष्ठ The lips. Abhī hoṅṭoṅ kā dūdh nahīṅ sūkhā . Prov. His mother's milk has not yet dried upon his lips. (He is as ignorant as a babe). hoṅṭ ūde paṛnā , v. n. Mīṭhe aur surd haiṅ itne ki zarā nām līye, Hoṅṭ chipke haiṅ, judā dāṅt haiṅ kaṛ kaṛ bajte . On melons. Nazīr. So sweet and cold that at the very name off went Your lips agglutinate, your teeth achattering sent. hoṅṭ chūsnā , v. a. To suck the lips. hoṅṭ hilānā , v. n. 1. To move one's lips. 2. To speak. 1. Ṭuk hoṅṭ hilāūṅ to yeh kahtā hai 'na bak be!' Aur pās jo baiṭhūṅ to sunātā hai 'sarak be!' Nazir. If I but move my lips she says, 'dont babble, fellow!' If I sit near her she exclaims, 'sit further, fellow!' 2. Hoṅṭ hale nā jibbhā kholī, phir bhī sās kahe baḍh-bolī . Rus. Prov. I never move my lips nor e'er my tongue thrust out, And yet my mother-in-law says, I'm a talking-spout. hoṅṭ al , adj. Thick-lipped. hoṅṭh'ī , n. f. A bit. hoṅṭhī akshar . s.m. The seat or faculty of thought and feeling; the heart; soul; mind; life; — the interior (of the body), breast, bosom; — knowledge; — affection
subs: s.m. An epithet of Kām-dev, the god of love
adj. (f. -vatī), Having a heart, tender-hearted, kind, humane
(˚ya+īś˚), s.m. 'Lord of the heart,' a husband
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit हृदय (hṛdaya), from Proto-Indo-European *ḱḗr.

def: adj. Good-hearted, tender-hearted, affectionate, kind, friendly.
adj. (f. -inī) = hr̤idayālu, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā, or -ī) = hr̤idayālu, q.v.

ety: S. हृद्+ईयः; or corr. of S. हृद्यः
def: adj. Of or belonging to the heart; proceeding from, or produced in, the heart; hearty, cordial.
s.m. Any organ of sense (syn. indrī)
subs: (˚ka+īś˚), s.m. 'Lord of the organs of sense,' i.e. Vishṇu, or Krishṇa.

ety: rt. हृष्
def: part. (f. -ā), Having the hair of the body bristling (with rapture), thrilled (with delight); enraptured, delighted, rejoiced, pleased, glad, cheerful, happy; — laughing, smiling; — astonished, surprised; — disappointed
subs: adj. Merry (or frisky) and fat, happy and well-fed; well-conditioned.

def: first-person singular present indicative/subjunctive of ہونا (honā)
pron. (he) God. hū haq h. , v. n. To be destroyed. hū kā ālam , n. m. A howling wilderness.
more: A voc. particle. Ho! I say! Miyāṅ hot! Ho there! hark ye! hallo there!

ety: Ap. Prk. ˚हू or ˚ह; Prk. ˚ह; S. ˚थ
def: imperat. (e.g. हसहु ( = हंसो) = Prk. हसहु or हसह = S. हसथ).
intj. 1˚ of calling; — 2˚ of pride; — 3˚ of contempt; — 4˚ of grief.
s.m. Noise, report, rumour, &c. (i.q. hūhā, q.v.).
adj. Cor. of A. huā huā . Exactly; precisely ( joṅ kā toṅ ).

ety: dialec.
def: part. 1˚ = hī, q.v.; — 2˚ = bhī, q.v. Aff. of the dat. & acc. case in old Hindī and in Braj; — also (in Poetry) the aff. of the ablative case ( = se).
more: pron. (he is) God. هوکاعالم hū kā āﻌlam, A desert plain (q. d. where no being but God exists). هوهو huwa huwa, Exactly.
adv. Too, exactly: see S̱. هون hūṅ. ¶ ho, 1. adv. (dakh.) Yes, ay, be it. 2. from होनौं in Braj ) Was.

ety: Ap. Prk. हुआ (for हुइआ), Prk. भविदओ = S. भव (fr. भू)+इत+कः
def: part. (of honā; f. huʼī), Become; (as a finite verb) became; has become; was; — happened; passed, or has passed (and hence it may often be rendered 'since; ago,' e.g. ek baras huʼā, 'a year ago')
more: past. part. or tense of (هونا) Become, became, was.
subs: '(What) has happened has happened'; let bygones be bygones.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀪𑀽𑀤 (bhūda), from Sanskrit भूत (bhūta, “become, been”), perfect participle of भवति (bhavati, “to become”).

def: adv. (dialec.) = wahāṅ
subs: adv. = wahāṅ-hī, 'in that very place, or in the very same place.'

def: adv. See s.v. هو huwa or hū. pron. He; — he is; — a name of the Deity
subs: s.m. A desert plain, a howling wilderness (q.d. where no being but God exists)
or hū-ba-hu, adv. Just as it is, exactly, precisely (syn. bi-ʻaini-hi; joṅ-kā toṅ).

def: s.m.(?) Name of an ancient idol in the temple of Mecca.
more: Name of an idol in the kaﻌba at Mecca before the time of Muhammadanism.

ety: fr. حبر 'to beautify,' &c.
def: s.m. The bustard.

ety: inf. n. of حب 'to be beloved'
def: s.f. Love, affection, friendship; inclination, wish, desire
more: n. f. حب was beloved. 1. ( In Comp .) Love; affection. 2. Means; resources; ability; power. Bābā, jitnī hub ho, utnā do! Mendicants. hubb-ul-watan ; Illit. hubb-ul-watanī , n. f. Love of one's country; patriotism. amal-i-hubb ; H. mohnī-mantr . A love charm.
(Heb. ) s. f. Love, affection, friendship. حب الوطن ḥubbu-l-wat̤an, Love of oneʼs country, patriotism.
subs: s.f. Love of one's country, patriotism
wety: From Arabic حَبّ (ḥabb).

def: s.f. pl. (of ḥabb), Grains (of corn, &c.); berries.
s.f.pl. (of ḥubūb), Vegetables, pot-herbs; articles formerly furnished gratis to men in authority, consisting of sheep, milk, eggs, blankets, hides, &c.

ety: inf. n. of هبط 'to fall, or descend (from a height
def: s.m. Falling, descending; fall, descent; decrease, &c. (i.q. habt̤, q.v.); — a descent, a declivity.
adj. (dakh.) Infatuated, mad ( Harris ).

ety: hūć˚, prob. = S. हूर्छ(ति), rt. हुर्छ 'to go crookedly'
def: v.n. To go wide (of the mark), to miss, to err, to mistake.
more: v. a. To miss, to err, to mistake.

def: s.m. = هدي hudā, q.v.
more: s. For هدي hudạ̄, q. v.

ety: inf. n. of هدي 'to guide, direct'; see hidāyat
def: s.m. Pointing out the right way; direction or guidance into the way of salvation; — the right path, the way of salvation.
more: s. m. Pointing out the right way, guidance in the right way. ¶ hadī, s. f. 1. Cattle carried to Mecca to be sacrificed. 2. Giving as an offering. 3. A bride. 4. A captive.

def: s.m. Truth; — adj. True, correct, just, right; — old.

ety: prob. fr. hūlā
def: s.m. (dialec.) A thrust.
v.n. = هڙکنا huṛaknā, q.v. v.n. To fret, to pine (i.q. haṛaknā); — to peep (i.q. hulaknā).
s.m. (Dakh.) A quagmire, slough (syn. daldal).
more: s. m. (dakh.) Quagmire: see دلدل daldal.

def: s.m. (dialec.) corr. of عہده ʻǒhda, q.v. intj. (generally applied to inferiors), Ho! ho there! holla;
subs: s.m. The letter or sound o.

ety: prob. S. हृद्+अकः; or fr. S. अष्ठि
def: s.m. (rustic or dialec.), Unripe grain; uncooked grain (having a hard kernel inside).
To threaten mutually.

ety: huddā, prob. = Prk. हुत्तिओ = S. हूति+कः, rt. व्हे
def: s.m. Calling defiantly (to one another), mutual threatening.
n. m. The woodpecker.
s.m. The hoopoe; — the lapwing, pewit.
more: s. m. Name of a bird. (Hoopoe).

ety: of ḥadd
def: s.f. The four boundaries
more: n. f. pl. of حد Boundaries. hudūd arbah ; H. chau-sīmā , n. The four boundaries. hudūd-i-arzī , n. Local limits.
s. plur. (of حدّ) 1. Boundaries, confines. 2. Penal laws.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حُدُود (hudūd), from Arabic حُدُود (ḥudūd), plural of حَدّ (ḥadd).

ety: inf. n. of حدث
def: s.m. The being new or recent; novelty; invention.
s. m. Novelty, the being new or recent, invention.
masculine plural perfective participle; masculine plural perfect indicative
wety: See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.

def: s.m. A kind of reed (com. called 'elephant-grass').
n. f. S. हाहा 1. Uproar; noise. 2. The hooting of an owl.

ety: prob. S. हू+हा
def: s.m. Noise, uproar, hue and cry; commotion; — a storm; — report, rumour, fame; pomp, pageantry, ostentation, parade; — hooting, hoot (of an owl)
more: s. m. 1. Report, rumour, popular fame. 2. A storm. 3. Pageantry, ostentation.
subs: v.n. To make a noise, &c.; — to whoop; to hoot.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = next, q.v.
s.m. (Myth.) One of the heavenly choristers (gandharv).

ety: Prk. होज्जहि, for होज्जहिसि = S.भविष्यसि, 2nd pers. sing. of fut. of rt. भू; or = S. भविष्यसे (as though भूयस्यसे), fut. pass. (used actively) of भू
def: imperat. 3rd pers. pl. (fr. honā), Please to be; kindly be; be (it is used with the honorific pronoun āp, or some equivalent word).

ety: hūjiye, q.v.+gā, the termn. of the future
def: imperat. (of honā), You will be pleased to be ( = hūjiye, q.v.). imperat. 3rd pers. pl. (fr. honā), Please to be; kindly be; be (it is used with the honorific pronoun āp, or some equivalent word).

ety: Prk. होज्जहिह or होज्जहिहु = S. भविष्यथ, 2nd pers. pl. of fut. of rt. भू (see hūjiye)
def: imperat. (of honā), Be you; see that you be (it is com. used with the pron. tum).
s.m. pl. (of ḥājib), Door-keepers, porters, &c.
more: s. plur. (of حاجب) Porters, door-keepers, &c. ¶ ḥajjāb, s. m. A chamberlain, door-keeper.

def: s.m. pl. (of ḥājj), Pilgrims (to Mecca).

ety: for A. حجة, v.n. fr. حجّ 'to overcome by an argument,' &c.
def: s.f. Argument, plea, allegation; proof, reason; objection, pretext, excuse; disputation, contention, altercation
more: n. f. حج argued. 1. See takrār , 2, 3, and بحث Hikmat-i-chīn, bujjat-i-Bangāl . Prov. China for art, Bengal for disputation. 2. Altercation; disputation. hujjat k., yā lānā , v. a. To call in question; challenge; demur; contest ( takrār k .); argue; plead; take exception to. hujjat-i-lā-tāil , n. f. A useless objection; cavil. hujjat nikāl'nā , v. a. To take exception; make objection. bilā hujjat . Undisputed; unchallenged.
s. f. 1. An argument, proof, reason. 2. Altercation, disputation.
subs: ḥujjat lānā, v.n. To adduce an argument or plea (against), take exception (to), to dispute, contest; to raise an objection, bring forward a pretext
s.f. A useless objection; a frivolous pretext; cavil

def: s.m. A sound reasoner; — a disputatious person, sophist, arguer, disputer, wrangler, caviller, disputant.
more: adj. Argumentative; contentious; disputatious ( takrārī ); quarrelsome ( jhagṛālū ). Hujjatī lā-ummatī . Prov. To argue is to be a sceptic. huj'jatī , n. m. A wrangler; disputant.
(from حجت) adj. used substantively. 1. A sound reasoner. 2. A disputatious person, a sophist, wrangler, caviller.
adj. used substantively, A disputer, disputant, arguer, brangler.

ety: for A. حجرة ḥujrat, fr. حجر; see ḥajar
def: s.m. A chamber, room, closet, cell; a hut.
more: n. m. A cell; closet; a small chamber; a private room (generally for worship). Hujrā bhī aur mujrā bhī! Prov. Saying. The pleasures of the dance, and the person too as well!
(from حجر) s. m. A cell, a closet, a chamber.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic حُجْرَة (ḥujra),

def: s.m.pl. of ḥujra, q.v.
n. m. ﮩجم driving in. A mob; concourse ( bhīṛ ). hujūm k. , v. n. To crowd; make a crowd.

ety: inf. n. of هجم 'to rusb (upon
def: s.m. Rushing (upon, or at, -par); attacking; crowding, swarming (round, or about, -par); — assault, attack; effort; impetuosity; — crowd, throng, concourse, mob; a swarm
more: (from هجم) s. m. A crowd, a mob, concourse, throng, an assault, attack, impetuosity. هجوم کرنا hujūm karnā, To rush (upon).
subs: (-par), To rush (upon, or at); to crowd, or swarm (round, or about); to form a crowd or mob; — to attack, assault, charge.
wety: From Arabic هُجُوم (hujūm).

def: corr. of حضور ḥuẓūr, q.v. s.m. Presence, attendance; the royal presence; the presence of a superior authority (as a judge, &c.); the person of the monarch or of any high functionary; — the presence chamber, hall of audience, the court; the government; government estate; — your Majesty, or Highness, &c.; — postpn. In the presence (of, -ke)
subs: s.f. The collection of revenue by the chief fiscal officer of the government without the intervention of a third party; — the taḥṣīl of the head-quarters of the collector of a district
adj. Being called to the presence; summoning attendance:-ḥuẓūr-maḥāl, s.m. Estates paying revenue direct to the government
s.m. Secretary of State, one who registers all royal or viceregal grants and orders: a private secretary
s.m. The high or exalted presence.

def: n. f. 1. A pain; stitch; shooting pain. 2. A cry of pain. 1. Kanchan se āg lagī, chunnī chingārī bhaī, dūkhan lāgo tan, bhūsan utārle! Bālam bides, āg lāge kyoṅ na? Aisī bais jar jar uṭhat hūk, tan birhā barāële, Āgī ke māṅgve ko par ghar kit jāt, ālī? āgī to aṅg meṅ, aṅgār do ek jhār le! Sāṅjh bhaī bhavan meṅ, sanjhvāt kyoṅ na bār, ālī, chhātī se chhuā diyā bātī kyoṅ na bār le? Kabit. This gold inflames me, rubies embers-hot burn me, My body aches, take off these jewels—misery! My husband far from me I needs must be on fire, In one so young burns strong the flame of keen desire, Why to another's house for fire d'you go, my dear? Sparks red-hot from my body you may draw off here, The shades of evening fall, the evening lamp now light! Touched by my burning breasts the wick will burn full bright. 2. Bhūk janam kī hūk . Rus. Prov. Hunger, life's torturer. hūk hūk-kar ronā ; Sant huṅk huṅkra , v. n. To sob and cry.
s.f. (dialec.) corr. of hūk, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. हू+कृ; see hūknā
def: s.f. Shooting pain, twitch, stitch; pain, ache (lit. 'that causes one to cry out hū, 'oh!'); — a cry of pain
more: s. f. Pain, stitch, ache, shooting pain, twitch. هوک هوک کررونا hūk hūk kar ronā, To weep with sobbing, or with repeated burstings forth.
subs: (or huk-huk-kar) ronā, To sob and cry; to weep with repeated bursts and sobs.

def: To burst forth again and again into tears (?).
s.m. pl. (of ḥakīm), Wise men, sages, philosophers; physicians.
more: s. plur. (of حکيم) Philosophers, sages, physicians.

ety: fr. hūknā-ćūknā, qq.v.
def: s.f. Missing and mistaking; erring and straying; mistakes, errors, blunders.

s.m. The Hindī corr. of حقه ḥuqqa, q.v. s.m. A casket, a small box, a small cocoanut used as a box; (in India) the hookah or pipe and its apparatus by which tobacco is smoked through water; — a sort of rocket (used in war), a grenade or bomb
subs: vulg. ḥuqqe-bāz, s.m. A juggler; — a grenadier; — a great smoker of the ḥuqqā
s.m. The servant whose business it is to attend to the ḥuqqa
To prepare the ḥuqqa, fill the ćilam, q.v.
(kisī-kā), To stop smoking and drinking with (one), to put (one) out of caste, to excommunicate (one)
To change the water of the ḥuqqa
s.m. The water of a ḥuqqa-bottom (which acquires a foul smell after the ḥuqqa has been smoked)
s.m. A great smoker of the ḥuqqa.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit हिक्का (hikkā). Doublet of ہِیک (hīk).

ety: for A. حقة ḥuqqat, fem.; fr. حقّ
def: s.m. A casket, a small box, a small cocoanut used as a box; (in India) the hookah or pipe and its apparatus by which tobacco is smoked through water; — a sort of rocket (used in war), a grenade or bomb
more: n. m. The hookah; the pipe, etc. in which tobacco is smoked. 1. Huqqā afīmī kā! Prov. No pipe like the opiumeater's! 2. Huqqā lagāë baiṭhā rahtā hai . He sits all day with the pipe in his mouth. 3. Huqqā bhar baṛe ko dīje, jab sulge tab āp bhī pīje . Prov. The huqqa filled to your superior give, when well-lighted then have your pull, its flavour you derive. 4. Huqqā ṭake gintā hai . The huqqa draws well. 5. Huqqā chār vaqt achchhā—so-ke, mūṅh dho-ke, khā-ke, nahā-ke; aur chār vaqt burā—aṅdhī meṅ, aṅdhere meṅ, bhūk meṅ, aur dhūp meṅ . Prov. A smoke is pleasant when you wake, and after a wash, and meals, and bath. It's not so nice in stormy weather, darkness, hunger, or the sun. 6. Huqqe se hurmat gaī, nem gayā sab chhūt, Pagṛī bech līyā tammākū, gaī hīye kī phūl . The hukka hath your honor lost, religion, virtue gone, Your turban for tobacco sold, all shame from you hath flown. 7. Huqqā h…
s. m. A casket or little box, a sort of rocket used in war, the pipe, &c. in which tobacco is smoked. حقه باز ḥuḳḳa-bāz, A juggler. حقه بردار ḥuḳḳa-bardār, The servant who prepares the ḥuḳḳa.
subs: vulg. ḥuqqe-bāz, s.m. A juggler; — a grenadier; — a great smoker of the ḥuqqā
s.m. The servant whose business it is to attend to the ḥuqqa
To prepare the ḥuqqa, fill the ćilam, q.v.
(kisī-kā), To stop smoking and drinking with (one), to put (one) out of caste, to excommunicate (one)
To change the water of the ḥuqqa
s.m. The water of a ḥuqqa-bottom (which acquires a foul smell after the ḥuqqa has been smoked)
s.m. A great smoker of the ḥuqqa.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حقه (huqqa), from Arabic حُقَّة (ḥuqqa)

ety: of ḥākim, q.v.
def: s.m. Governors, rulers, commanders, authorities, officers, judges, magistrates
more: n. m. plur. of حاکم Rulers; governors; commanders; authorities; officers; magistrates. hukkām-i-bā'ā-dast . Superior officers or authorities. hukkām-i-zila . District officers; local authorities. hukkām-i-faujdārī . Magisterial authorities; criminal officers. hukkām-i-mātahat yā tābe , Subordinate officers. hukkām-i-māl . Revenue authorities.
s. plur. (of حاکم) Governours, rulers, commanders.
subs: s.m. Magisterial authorities, criminal judges or magistrates

ety: rel. n. fr. ḥukkām
def: adj. Of or pertaining to, or done by, rulers, &c.; — s.f. Grants of land made by the officers of government.
direct plural; oblique/vocative singular

ety: inf. n. of حکم 'to prevent or restrain'
def: s.m. Judgment, judicial decision, sentence, decree, verdict, doom, award; judicial authority, jurisdiction, rule, dominion, government, control, direction, management; — an ordinance, a statute, a prescript, edict, decree, law, enactment, precept, rule, predicament; an order, a command; sanction, permission, a requisition; — effect, influence, efficiency; article (of faith, &c.); — the firs card thrown by rule (in a game)
more: n. m. Pl. ahkām . 1. Order; injunction; sentence. 1. Hukm kī der hai . Give the order and it is done. 2. Hukm-i-hākim marg-i-mufājāt . Prov. The ruler's order is sudden death. 3. Hukm ke sāth sab kuchh maujūd hai . Prov. All things wait on authority. 2. Provision; stipulation; enactment. 3. Jurisdiction; authority; legal or executive authority. Hukm banā rahe! Benediction. May your authority endure! 4. Direction; control; management. 5. Leave; sanction; permission; license. 6. Decision; judgment; finding; decree; award; verdict; sentence. 7. ( hukm-nāmā ) A warrant. 8. A rule; law; precept. 9. A call; demand; requisition. 10. The first card thrown by rule in a game. hukm uṭhā'nā , v. a. 1. To countermand or cancel an order. 2. To cut for the deal at ganjfā. hukm-i-akhīr , A final order. hukm akhīr denā v. a. 1. To issue final orders; to pass a final judgment. 2. To settle finally. hukm u…

s. m. Order, command, decree, sentence, doom, award, authority, jurisdiction, precept, effect, influence, efficiency, permission, article (of faith, &c.). (Plur. احکام). حکم اٿهانا يا نکالنا ḥukm-uṭhānā or -nikālnā, To draw at cards. حکم انداز ḥukm-andāz, Hitting the mark aimed at, obedient to directions. حکم ران ḥukm-rān (حکم with the part. act. of راندن To drive) Ruling, exercising sovereignty. حکم راني ḥukm-rān-ī, s. f. Sway, sovereignty. حکم کرنا ḥukm karnā, To command, order, adjudge, award, sentence, decree. حکم کش ḥukm-kash, Under command, obedient to orders. حکم نامه ḥukm-nāma, s. m. Written orders or decree.
subs: (-ka), To execute or carry out an order; — to countermand or cancel an order; — to draw (at cards); to cut for the deal
s.m. A final order or judgment
(-kā), To reverse the judgment or decision (of), to countermand an order
adj. & s.m. Obedient to orders or directions; — hitting the mark aimed at; — a good marksman, a sure shot
(-ke), To act according to orders, to obey or execute an order
(-ke), At the bidding or disposal (of); pending the orders (of)
(-kā), To infringe or violate an order, to disobey
To exercise authority, to rule or govern
s.m. An intermediate or interlocutory order
v.t. To pass judgment or sentence; to decide, to sentence; to give or pass an order, to order, direct, enjoin, issue an injunction; to authorize; to permit, sanction
s.f. Rule, sway, sovereignty, government, administration
(-kā), To exercise the authority (of), hold the place or position (of)
s.m. Summons for the appearance (of persons); application for a decree or judgment (in a court of law)
s.m. An order written on the back (of a writing)
s.m. A decisive or imperative or final order; a clear or explicit command or injunction
To sentence, decree, adjudge, award; to rule, govern, to exercise authority; to order, command, direct, enjoin
adj. Obedient to orders, under command
(-kā), To affirm or assert positively; — to foretell, to predict
s.m. Order of dismissal (of a person); — arrest of judgment
(-ke), To abide or be under the authority or rule (of); to stand in the predicament (of), conform to the rule (of)
(-kā), To disallow or to overrule an order (of another)
s.m. A decisive or peremptory order
s.m. Written order, writ, decree, warrant, written award or judgment, injunction, process, prescript; — a deed conveying certain authority, a license
Whatever your order it shall be obeyed (by me), I await your orders, your servant to command.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حُکْم (hukm), borrowed from Arabic حُكْم (ḥukm).

def: Enforced transfer.

ety: rel. n. fr. ḥukm
def: adj. Obedient to directions (generally applied to medicine).
more: adj. 1. Unfailing; infallible; certain. 2. Enforced; imperative; compulsory; coercive. 3. Obedient; dutiful. 4. Having a good eye; hitting the mark. Hukmī tīr lagātā hai . hukmī dāvā , n. f. A certain specific. hukmī vazٴa . Authorised deductions. hukmī-banda , n. m. An obedient or dutiful person. Hukmī bandah jannat meṅ . Prov. Obedient on earth, a place in Heaven. hukm'ī , adv. Positively; surely; certainly ( bilāshak ).
adj. Obedient to directions. (Applied to medicine).

ety: prob. hūk˚ = Prk. हुक्क(इ) = S. हू+कृ
def: v.n. To cry or sob from pain; to cry; sob; to fret, pine.

ety: cf. hūćnā, and ćūknā
def: v.n. To go or fall wide (of the mark), to fail to hit, to miss; to make a mistake; to err; to slip.

ety: for A. حقنة ḥuqnat, fem.; fr. حقن
def: s.m. A clyster; a clyster-pipe, a syringe, a squirt.
more: (from حقن) s. m. A syringe, a squirt, a clyster-pipe, a clyster.

def: n. m. pl. of حق Rights; claims; privileges; fees; dues; duties. huqūq-i-zaujīyat . Conjugal rights. huqūq-i-shauharī . Marital rights.
more: s. m. plur. (of حقّ) Rights, dues, just claims, laws.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حُقُوق (huqūq), from Arabic حُقُوق (ḥuqūq). First attested in c. 1707 as Middle Hindi حقوق (ḥqvq /⁠huqūq⁠/).

ety: of ḥaqq
def: s.m. Conjugal rights

ety: for A. حکومة, inf. n. of حکم 'to restrain,' &c.
def: s.f. Judicial authority; jurisdiction authority, power, sway, dominion, rule, sovereignty, government
more: n. f. حکم order. Power; sway; rule; administration; government; sovereignty ( hukm , 1, 4). 1. Hukūmat kī ghoṛī, aur chhaë paserī dānā . Prov. For the ruler's horse thirty seers of gram. (Authorities on tour, the country plundered sure.) 2. Jo kām hikmat se nikaltā hai, woh hukūmat se nahīṅ nikaltā . Prov. Skill succeeds where authority fails. 2. Province; dominion; domain; jurisdiction. hukūmat-i-aṅgrezī . British rule. hakūmat jatānā , v. n. To show off one's authority. hukūmat-i-sha/?/khsī , n. f. Monarchy; despotic government. hukūmat k. , v. a. To exercise authority; rule; govern; administer; dispense justice. hukūmat ke zor se zabar-dastī k . Oppressive exercise of authority; undue exertion of executive power. hukūmat yā riyāsat jamhūrī . A republic; democracy. ilm-i-hukūmat ; H. rājnītī , n. f. Jurisprudence.
(from حکم) s. f. Dominion, sovereignty, government, jurisdiction, sway, authority. حکومت کرنا ḥukūmat karnā, To govern, to rule.
subs: To make a display of authority
s.f. Dominion or sway of an individual; monarchy; despotic government
(-par), To exercise authority (over); to rule, govern
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حُکُومَت (hukūmat), borrowed from Arabic حُكُومَة (ḥukūma), from حَكَمَ (ḥakama).

def: the Government of India
v. n. To breathe hard with hunger or agony.
n. f. S. शूल A thurst; stab. hūl denā, hūlnā , v. a. 1. To goad; thrust; stab. 2. To impel; urge.
more: n. f. Terror; horror. haul baiṭh jānā , v. n. To be terrified, horrified. Bachche ke jī meṅ haul baiṭh gaī. haul-dil , n. f. Palpitation. haul-dilā , n. m. A timid person; a coward. haul dilī , n. f. A stone worn over the chest as a cure for palpitation. haul-nāk , adj. Dreadful; horrid ( bhayānak , 1).

ety: fr. hūlnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Thrust, probe; stab; a pass (in fencing); an attack.
more: s. f. A thrust, stab, an attack. هول دينا hūl denā, v. a. To goad, thrust, push, drive, impel, urge.

def: s.m. = hūl, q.v. s.f. Thrust, probe; stab; a pass (in fencing); an attack.
s.f. = hulhulī, q.v.
s.f. Inarticulalate sounds of pleasure (made by women).

ety: by redupl. fr. hulaknā, q.v.
def: s.f. Peeping; playing at bo-beep

ety: Prk. हुड्डक्के(इ), fr. S. हूड्+कृ; cf. hūlnā
def: v.t. To rush upon, to attack, to charge; — to search; — v.n. To peep (i.q. huṛaknā).
v.n. To peep (i.q. hulaknā, q.v. v.t. To rush upon, to attack, to charge; — to search; — v.n. To peep (i.q. huṛaknā).
s.m. = hullaṛ, q.v. s.m. A crowd; — noise, uproar, tumult, commotion, bustle, hurly-burly, disturbance, riot, row, mutiny; alarm.
more: s. m. Tumult, uproar, alarm, disturbance.

def: n. f. 1. (S. उल्लास ) Gladness; joy. 2. Snuff. Sūṅgh hulās, miṭe nakbhās . Rus. Take snuff, banish cold. hulās lenā, yā sūṅghnā , v. n. To take snuff. hulās-dānī . A snuff-box! hulās honā , v. n. To be consumed, finished.

ety: Prk. उल्लासो; S. उल्लासः, rt. लस् with उद्
def: s.m. Alacrity, gladness, joy, happiness; — s.f. Snuff
subs: or hulās lenā, To take snuff
To vanish, disappear; to be dissipated, to be consumed; to be finished.

ety: hulās, q.v.+Prk. अओ and इओ; S. अकः and इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. Gladdened, glad, rejoiced, delighted, happy.
adj. (dialec.) = hulasit, q.v.; — s.f. Name of the mother of Tulsī-dās.

ety: S. उल्लसितः; see hulasnā
def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Gladdened, rejoiced, delighted; glad, cheerful, happy.
v. n. See hulsānā , v. a.

ety: hulas˚ = Prk. उल्लस(इ) = S. उल्लस(ति), rt. लस् with उद्
def: v.n. To be gladdened, be rejoiced, be pleased or delighted, be cheered, &c. (see hulsānā); to rejoice.
more: v. n. To be rejoiced, pleased, delighted.

ety: hulās, q.v.+Prk. अओ and इओ; S. अकः and इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. Gladdened, glad, rejoiced, delighted, happy.

ety: for A. حلبة ḥulbat, fem.; fr. حلب 'to milk'
def: s.m. The plant fenugreek, Trigonella fœnungrœcum, (syn. methī).
more: s. The plant fenugreek. (In Hindī ميتهي).

def: s.m. The medicinal plants Cleome viscosa, &c. (i.q. hurhur, q.v.); — name of a small culinary herb which springs up in the rainy season.
more: s. m. Name of a plant. See هرهر hurhur.

def: s.m. Snow.

n. m. A small culinary herb which springs up in the rainy season. The commonest kind has a white flower and produces a long pod like that of the mūṅg . There are said to be four kinds, white, red, purple, and yellow; Gynandropsis pentaphylla , formerly Cleome- pentaphylla . Elliot.
s.f. pl. (of حَليٌ, rt. حلي), Ornaments (of a woman), jewels, gems.
more: (from حلا Heb. To polish) s. f. Jewels, ornaments.

ety: huliy˚, for hūlī (fr. hūl-nā, q.v.
def: v.t. To gore; to butt.
s.f. (dialec.) An epithet of the goddess Kālī (i.q. hulkī).
v. a. To set on (as a dog) ( lashkārnā ).

ety: see hulaknā
def: v.t. To incite, to set on (as cocks, &c., to fight), to halloo on (as a dog on a bull, &c.).
more: v. a. To set on (as a dog on a bull), to instigate, to halloo on.

def: n. f. An epidemic; a plague.

ety: fr. hulaknā
def: s.f. Wrath, choler; — an epidemic, a plague; — an epithet of the goddess Kālī (i.q. hulkar).
s.m. The windpipe; the throat ( = ḥalq).

ety: for A. حلة ḥullat, fem.; fr. حلّ 'to untie,' &c.
def: s.m. A dress consisting of a waist-wrapper and a wrapper for the whole body; a robe, garment.
more: s. m. A robe, a garment.

ety: perhaps Prk. हुड्डडो or हुल्लडो; S. होड (rt. हुड्)+रः
def: s.m. A crowd; — noise, uproar, tumult, commotion, bustle, hurly-burly, disturbance, riot, row, mutiny; alarm.
more: s. m. Alarm, tumult, uproar, commotion, bustle, disturbance, riot.

ety: hūl˚ = Prk. हूड(इ) or हूडे(इ) = S. हूडय(ति), caus. of rt. हूड्
def: v.t. To drive (as an ox, or an elephant), to urge forward; to impel; to goad; to thrust; to push; to stab; — to rush upon, to attack; — v.n. To thrust (at, -par); — to rush (upon)
more: v. a. To goad, to thrust, to push, to drive (an elephant).

ety: hulsā(nā) + āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Gladdening, rejoicing, cheering, encouraging, &c.
v. a. S. उल्लस् to cheer. 1. To cheer; rejoice; delight. 2. To inspire hope. 3. To stir up; excite; stimulate. Hulsā hulsā-ke laṛvā dīyā .

ety: caus. of hulasnā
def: v.t. To gladden, delight, rejoice, exhilarate, cheer; to encourage, inspirit, inspire with hope of; — to stir up, excite, stimulate.
more: (causal of هلسنا from उल्लास ) v. a. To cheer, rejoice, delight, exhilarate, glad.

def: n. m. Vomit. Ek hulūk ḍālte hī mar gayā .

ety: inf. n. of حلّ 'to alight,' &c.
def: s.m. Alighting, descent, stopping, sojourning; entering, penetrating; transmigration.
more: s. m. 1. Descent. 2. Stopping or sojourning. 3. Entering or penetrating. 4. Transmigration.

def: aff. pron., 3rd pers. pl. (pl. of huwa), They; them.
ex: ہَم سے نا کَبھی جُدا ہونا۔
ham se nā kabhī judā honā.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi हम (hama), हंम (haṃma), from Apabhramsa अम्हे (amhe), from Prakrit 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (amhe), from Sanskrit अस्मे (asme).

def: n. m, 1. A phœnix. 2. A bird of happy omen; an eagle. It is supposed that the head it overshadows will one day wear a orown. (Comp. legend of Tarquin).
